fact: nASR input shape: (B, C, W) = batch, channels, window samples
K_Offset >= 0.1, default 0.1
K_init = 0.9^2 - K_Offset (corresponds to 90% normalized std)
tau_D = 20 (STE temperature for Discard)
tau_L = 20 (STE temperature for Noise)
epsilon = 1e-6 (NormDiff denominator floor)
Clean reference threshold: mu +/- 3.5*sigma
28 channels from 9 ROIs out of 59 available
Sliding window: 256 samples (2.56s), step 20 samples (200ms)
Min resolvable frequency: ~0.39 Hz with 256 samples
Butterworth BPF: 6th-order, 0.5-30 Hz
EMG dominates EEG above ~25 Hz
Adjacency matrix: L2 distance < 0.05 in 10-20 system
STE enables hard binary decisions forward, smooth gradients backward
K governs artifact detection in PC variance; L quantifies eigen-spread for channel-level classification
nASR reconstructs channels, not PC subspace - preserves clean channels
asrpy version 0.0.8 for baseline ASR, threshold=20
BCI Competition IV Dataset 1, 100 Hz version, subjects a and f
Adam optimizer, lr=0.001, clipnorm=1.0, dropout=20%, batch=64, max 250 epochs
Loss = 0.5*BCE + 0.5*Dice; LR halved after 5 epochs plateau, min 1e-7; early stop at 50 epochs
Dual GPU: 2x NVIDIA RTX 6000 Ada, 48GB VRAM each, Python 3.11.15, TF 2.16.1, Keras 3.14.0
